title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate President supports a bill to end biannual clock changes and adopt permanent Atlantic standard time.
An informal Boston.com poll shows 83% of over 470 readers want to stop clock changes, despite disagreements on time system.
Concerns about the proposal include darker winter mornings and potential issues with neighboring states about time alignment.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham, Massachusetts, was arrested after driving 118 mph and crashing in New Hampshire.
The teenager led police on a pursuit through several towns before crashing into a retaining wall in Stratham.
Charges include multiple felonies and misdemeanors after showing signs of impairment and refusing to stop for police.

title: The ‘World’s Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade’ is still New England’s nuttiest St. Pat’s fever dream
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Little Compton, Rhode Island hosts 'World's Shortest St. Paddy’s Day Parade,' measuring 89 feet, every March.
The parade draws over 2,000 attendees annually, featuring quirky participants like people dressed as potatoes and fish.
Proceeds from the event support food banks, raising over $10K this year for communities in Rhode Island and Massachusetts.

title: Massachusetts ER doctors say long wait times reflect a health system under strain
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts ER delays are driven by staffing shortages, aging population, and limited hospital beds, according to local doctors.
Massachusetts ranks near the top nationwide for ER wait times, with patients averaging 189 minutes before leaving.
ERs are overburdened as they handle both true emergencies and patients unable to access primary care elsewhere.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about heavy rain, damaging winds on Monday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Heavy rain and thunderstorms expected in Boston area, causing potential flooding, with 1-3 inches of rain forecasted.
Strong winds with gusts between 45-65 mph anticipated, prompting High Wind Warnings and Wind Advisories.
Meteorologists advise flood-prone areas to prepare as rain and winds peak tonight, impacting low-lying regions.

title: Firefighter suffers cardiac arrest during Hyde Park blaze
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A firefighter suffered cardiac arrest during a Hyde Park warehouse fire on Sunday night, officials reported.
Firefighters responded around 8 p.m. to flames at 1 Westinghouse Plaza, requiring additional units to assist.
The blaze caused approximately $750,000 in damage; Boston Fire Department is investigating the fire's cause.

title: Mass. native’s 44-point performance helped Penn reach NCAA Tournament in dramatic fashion
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: T.J. Power, a Shrewsbury native, scored 44 points to help Penn upset Yale 88-84 in the Ivy League final.
Power's performance included a tying three-pointer, securing Penn's place in the NCAA Tournament as a 14-seed.
Penn will face third-seeded Illinois in the NCAA Tournament first round on Thursday at 9:25 p.m.

title: Hundreds of flights delayed or cancelled at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: More than 400 flights were delayed or cancelled due to severe weather at Logan Airport on Monday.
Massport officials advised passengers to check flight status amid a high wind warning for eastern Massachusetts.
Airlines including JetBlue, Delta, and Southwest are waiving change fees and allowing flexible rebooking options.

title: 11 charged after allegedly staging robberies to claim victim status on immigration applications
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Eleven charged in Massachusetts for allegedly staging robberies to claim U Visa victim status in immigration applications.
Defendants, all named Patel, staged armed robberies at Massachusetts businesses, spectating as robbery victims for U Visa benefits.
Rambhai Patel and accomplice Balwinder Singh orchestrated crimes; Patel sentenced to over 20 months in prison for fraud scheme.

title: One injured in South End shooting
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A South End shooting on Sunday evening injured one person near Washington and East Berkeley Streets.
The victim was transported to a local hospital with non-life-threatening injuries, authorities reported.
Boston Police have not made any arrests and the investigation into the shooting is ongoing.
